description:
Advances: mutagen.cli

Deliverable:
- New `test/integration/archive_install_test.exs` paralleling `test/integration/downstream_adoption_test.exs` but for archive-context adoption. Module tag: `@moduletag :archive_integration` (distinct from `:downstream_integration` so the default `mix test` lane skips both).
- Test body (sequenced):
  1. Compute archive filename from `MutagenEx.MixProject.project()[:version]` (do NOT hard-code `0.1.0`).
  2. Create a hermetic per-run scratch dir `Path.join(System.tmp_dir!(), "mutagen_ex_archives_test_#{rand}")` and pass it as `MIX_ARCHIVES=<dir>` in every `System.cmd` env. This makes the test NEVER touch the host's `~/.mix/archives/`.
  3. `System.cmd("mix", ["archive.build"], cd: @project_root, env: clean_env())` to produce the `.ez` in `@project_root`.
  4. `System.cmd("mix", ["archive.install", "--force", "./<archive>.ez"], cd: @project_root, env: scoped_env(scratch_dir))` to install into the scoped dir.
  5. `System.cmd("mix", ["new", app_name], cd: System.tmp_dir!(), env: clean_env())` for the host project. Do NOT patch `mix.exs` to add `:mutagen_ex` â€” the whole point is no dep declaration.
  6. Write a trivial `Calc` module + test file (same shape as `downstream_adoption_test.exs`).
  7. `System.cmd("mix", ["mutagen", "--scope", ..., "--tests", ...], cd: tmp_dir, env: scoped_env(scratch_dir))` to drive the task.
  8. Extract the JSON document from stdout using `extract_json_document/1` + `json_parses?/1` helpers (duplicate from `downstream_adoption_test.exs` OR extract to `test/support/integration_helpers.ex` â€” implementer picks; duplication is acceptable for two callers).
  9. Assert: `mutagen_code == 0`, decoded JSON has `aborted == false`, `abort_reason in [nil, :null]`, `is_map(mutation)`, `mutation["total"] > 0`.
- `on_exit` cleanup: `File.rm_rf!(scratch_dir)`, `File.rm_rf!(tmp_dir)`, and remove the built `.ez` from `@project_root`.
- One-line edit to `mix.exs` `aliases/0` so `mix test.integration` includes the new tag â€” change
  `"test.integration": "test --include downstream_integration test/integration"` to
  `"test.integration": "test --include downstream_integration --include archive_integration test/integration"`.

Spec touches:
- None (the test IS the verification artifact for `mutagen.cli.r16`; verification stub `v11` was authored by /distill and currently has `execute: false` â€” flip to `true` in this stage once the test file lands).
- Confirm `v11`'s `command` field matches the test path: `mix test test/integration/archive_install_test.exs --include archive_integration`.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: `mix test test/integration/archive_install_test.exs --include archive_integration` (1 test, 0 failures) AND `mix test.integration` (both `:downstream_integration` and `:archive_integration` pass).
- Behaviors to demonstrate: scenario `mutagen.cli.s14c` end-to-end â€” the failing-tuple repair fires, the pipeline runs normally, and the emitted JSON document has `aborted: false`.
- Mechanical: `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` clean; `mix smoke` green (default lane still excludes the new tag).
- Spec validation: `mix spec.validate` green.

Out of Scope (files):
- `test/integration/downstream_adoption_test.exs` (MUST NOT be edited â€” its behaviour is the byte-for-byte parallel reference; any change risks regressing the existing gate)
- `lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex` (owned by S1)
- `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md` (owned by S3)
- Any other `lib/` source files

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- `test/integration/archive_install_test.exs` (new file)
- `mix.exs` (the `aliases/0` line only â€” single character-range change to add `--include archive_integration`)
- `test/support/integration_helpers.ex` (new file, OPTIONAL â€” only if extracting shared `extract_json_document/1` / `json_parses?/1` helpers from `downstream_adoption_test.exs`)
- `.spec/specs/cli.spec.md` (one-line flip: `v11.execute: false` â†’ `true`)

Out of Scope (intent):
- No new third-party deps. OTP's `:json.decode/1` is the JSON parser, matching the existing downstream-adoption test.
- The test MUST be hermetic â€” no leftover archive in the host's `~/.mix/archives` after the test exits. Scoped `MIX_ARCHIVES` is the chosen mechanism; `mix archive.uninstall` is an alternative but still touches the user's global dir mid-run.
- Do NOT add a new `test_helper.exs` configuration block. The existing `test_helper.exs` already excludes `:downstream_integration` by default; `:archive_integration` joins that exclusion list â€” confirm and add if missing.

Merge gate:
- [ ] `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` clean
- [ ] `mix test.integration` green (both tags pass)
- [ ] `mix smoke` green (default lane still skips integration tests)
- [ ] `mix spec.validate` green; `v11.execute` flipped to `true`
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: codex-3a5e7c19
Log: /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-archive-install-test/.codex-runs/mutagen-wrd.40.2.log

Commit: f2834abc9d6e395bf4fff472d6c48e4952594215
Files touched: test/integration/archive_install_test.exs, mix.exs, test/test_helper.exs, .spec/specs/cli.spec.md
Tests added: MutagenEx.Integration.ArchiveInstallTest test "fresh-shell `mix mutagen` succeeds when mutagen_ex is installed as an archive"
Verification stubs flipped: mutagen.cli.v11

Summary: Added the archive-install integration test for mutagen.cli.r16. The test builds the current versioned .ez archive, installs it into a hermetic MIX_ARCHIVES directory, runs a generated host project with no mutagen_ex dependency, and asserts the emitted JSON completes with aborted false and mutation.total > 0.

VERIFY: FAIL
Session: 90ec767b-9d9f-4997-9c96-09f1db6d916f
Log: ~/.claude/sidecar/90ec767b-9d9f-4997-9c96-09f1db6d916f/events.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 4 (test/integration/archive_install_test.exs, mix.exs, test/test_helper.exs, .spec/specs/cli.spec.md)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 1 â€” test/test_helper.exs is not in the ticket's "Allowed touches" list but Out-of-Scope (intent) authorizes "add :archive_integration to the existing exclusion list â€” confirm and add if missing." The diff does exactly that (line 36 adds :archive_integration to the ExUnit.start exclude list); the extra comment block on lines 30-35 is documentation only. Intent-level scope authorizes this; flagging as advisory.
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0 (downstream_adoption_test.exs untouched; lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex untouched; README/CHANGELOG untouched)

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 1 (mutagen.cli.v11, execute: true), passed: 0
- Requirements coverage:
  - mutagen.cli.r16: NOT COVERED â€” the verification stub command `mix test test/integration/archive_install_test.exs --include archive_integration` hangs indefinitely on `System.cmd("mix", ["archive.build"], cd: @project_root, ...)`. Reproduced at 60s, 120s, 300s, and 600s test timeouts â€” every run terminates with `ExUnit.TimeoutError` at archive_install_test.exs:39 (the `mix archive.build` call). The child `mix archive.build` BEAM starts (visible as a child pid under the parent `mix test` BEAM) and produces no output for the entire timeout window. Direct CLI `mix archive.build` from a fresh shell completes in <1s, and the same `System.cmd` invocation from `mix run /tmp/foo.exs` (no ExUnit parent) completes in ~450ms, so the failure is specific to nested mix invocations against `cd: @project_root` from inside a running ExUnit BEAM â€” almost certainly Mix's `with_build_lock` / project-stack contention. As written, the test cannot complete in this environment; the verification stub it is the single artifact for produces zero pass/fail signal for r16.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none (no new third-party deps in mix.exs; OTP `:json.decode/1` reused; MIX_ARCHIVES is per-run scoped â€” host's ~/.mix/archives never written to)
- Project verification: `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` clean. `mix test test/integration/downstream_adoption_test.exs --include downstream_integration` still green (2.6s, 1 test 0 failures â€” no regression to the parallel reference). `mix spec.validate` not available in this worktree (spec_led_development dep not installed).

Verdict: FAIL

Gaps:
- mutagen.cli.r16 / mutagen.cli.s14c: The deliverable (test/integration/archive_install_test.exs) is the verification artifact named by stub v11. With v11 flipped to execute: true, that stub now fails on every run â€” the test hangs at step 3 (mix archive.build) and never reaches the s14c assertion. r16's "the failing-tuple repair fires, the pipeline runs normally, the emitted JSON has aborted: false, mutation.total > 0" is not demonstrated by any passing test. The downstream-adoption test exercises the dep-style branch (r14) but explicitly does not enter the repair branch â€” r16's repair code path has no green signal anywhere.
- Stub-suite green is now strictly worse than at start-of-stage: v11 was execute: false (vacuously fine) and is now execute: true and failing, so `mix spec.check` (and any orchestrator that runs the stub suite) would regress on this stage.

Notes:
- The hang is structurally caused by `cd: @project_root` for all three child mix invocations (archive.build, archive.install). The parallel reference (downstream_adoption_test.exs) only ever runs child mix with `cd: tmp_dir` against the generated downstream project â€” it never invokes child mix against @project_root, which is why it does not hit this contention. A workable design would build the .ez out-of-band (e.g. via a Mix.Task.run("archive.build") in-process during setup, or invoke the child mix against a temporary clone of the project root) but neither shape is what shipped.
- test/test_helper.exs touch is authorized by Out of Scope (intent)'s "confirm and add if missing" clause for :archive_integration. The added 5-line comment block is advisory documentation â€” not flagged as a scope violation.
- mix.exs aliases edit matches the ticket spec exactly (adds --include archive_integration). The v11.execute flip is correct. Both Allowed.

AUDIT: BOUNCE
Session: 90ec767b-9d9f-4997-9c96-09f1db6d916f/subagents/agent-aeabaa8ad2f4b2650
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/90ec767b-9d9f-4997-9c96-09f1db6d916f/subagents/agent-aeabaa8ad2f4b2650.jsonl

Reasoning: Independently reproduced the verifier's diagnosis. Running `mix test test/integration/archive_install_test.exs --include archive_integration` from inside the stage worktree fails after 60s with `ExUnit.TimeoutError` at `archive_install_test.exs:39` (the `System.cmd("mix", ["archive.build"], cd: @project_root, ...)` call) â€” confirmed via `perl -e 'alarm(120); exec @ARGV' mix test ...`. Direct shell `mix archive.build` in the same worktree completes in ~0.5s, confirming the hang is specific to nested `cd: @project_root` child-mix invocations from inside a running ExUnit BEAM (Mix `with_build_lock` / project-stack contention). Mechanical counts re-checked against `git diff 5a958ceb..HEAD --name-only`: exactly 4 files (test/integration/archive_install_test.exs, mix.exs, test/test_helper.exs, .spec/specs/cli.spec.md); `test/test_helper.exs` is outside the literal Allowed-touches list but the ticket's Out-of-Scope (intent) authorizes adding `:archive_integration` to the exclusion list â€” the diff does exactly that at line 36, so the verifier's "advisory only" routing is correct. Confirmed v11 was `execute: false` at the base commit (`git show 5a958ce:.spec/specs/cli.spec.md`) and is now `execute: true` and red â€” the stub suite is strictly worse than start-of-stage, exactly as the verifier called out. `mix test test/integration/downstream_adoption_test.exs --include downstream_integration` re-ran green in 2.5s â€” no regression to the parallel reference. r16's repair-branch behavior has no other green test gating it in this diff, so r16 / s14c are uncovered.

BOUNCE â†’ implementer: The structural choice of `cd: @project_root` for `System.cmd("mix", ["archive.build"], ...)` (test line 33-37) inside a `mix test`-spawned ExUnit BEAM deadlocks on Mix's build lock. The deliverable cannot complete in this environment and produces zero r16 / s14c signal. As the verifier notes, a workable shape is to build the `.ez` out-of-band (e.g. `Mix.Task.run("archive.build")` in-process at setup, or shell out to `mix archive.build` against a transient clone of `@project_root` outside the running project tree). The `mix.exs` aliases edit and `v11.execute` flip are correct and should stay; the test body is what needs the redesign.

IMPLEMENTED
Session: codex-3a5e7c20
Log: /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-archive-install-test/.codex-runs/mutagen-wrd.40.2-r2.log

Commit: ae68d59c6fd915a6efe9f6f0ac6745a70a49e305
Files touched: test/integration/archive_install_test.exs, mix.exs, test/test_helper.exs, .spec/specs/cli.spec.md
Tests added: MutagenEx.Integration.ArchiveInstallTest test "fresh-shell `mix mutagen` succeeds when mutagen_ex is installed as an archive"
Verification stubs flipped: mutagen.cli.v11
Audit-expanded scope: test/test_helper.exs â€” prior AUDIT: BOUNCE â†’ implementer comment at 2026-05-18T03:12:51Z confirmed the ticket intent authorizes adding :archive_integration to the default exclusion list.

Summary: Redesigned the archive-install integration test body to avoid nested child Mix invocations against the active project root. The test now builds the current versioned archive in-process with Mix.Task.run("archive.build"), installs the absolute .ez into a hermetic MIX_ARCHIVES directory from a neutral temp working directory, then runs a generated host project with no :mutagen_ex dependency and asserts the JSON report completed with aborted false and mutation.total > 0.

AUDIT: BOUNCE â†’ orchestrator (scope expansion)

Session: 90ec767b-9d9f-4997-9c96-09f1db6d916f
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/90ec767b-9d9f-4997-9c96-09f1db6d916f.jsonl

Reasoning: Verifier's diagnosis confirmed cold. Reproduced `mix test test/integration/archive_install_test.exs --include archive_integration` â€” the subprocess `mix mutagen` raises `FunctionClauseError` in `:code.add_pathz/2` because `lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:322` calls `:code.add_pathz(ebin)` where `ebin = Mix.Local.archive_ebin(archive)` returns a binary (`"/var/folders/.../mutagen_ex-0.1.0/ebin"`), but `:code.add_pathz/1` requires a charlist (OTP `:code` API). Stack trace exactly matches: `(mutagen_ex 0.1.0) lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:276` â†’ `Enum.find_value_list/3` â†’ `lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:322`. The S2 test body (`test/integration/archive_install_test.exs`) is structurally correct: hermetic scratch `MIX_ARCHIVES`, all `mix` invocations as `System.cmd` subprocesses with scoped env (no mix-in-mix lock deadlock), correct on_exit cleanup, matches the ticket deliverable spec. `git diff 5a958ce..HEAD -- lib/ test/mutagen_ex/` is empty â€” the `ast_test.exs:135` smoke failure is a base-ref pre-existing condition, not introduced by S2. `lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex` is explicitly in S2's Out-of-Scope (files) list ("owned by S1"), so S2 cannot legitimately fix this.

New ticket title: "S1b: archive ebin charlist cast for `:code.add_pathz/1` (r16 MatchError/FunctionClauseError leak)"
File to fix: `lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex`
Exact line: `lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:322`
Contract violation: r16's "MUST NOT raise a `MatchError` or leak a traceback" clause â€” the repair branch currently raises `FunctionClauseError` (same family of contract violation: an unhandled exception with traceback escapes from the runtime preamble instead of being caught and routed through `handle_runtime_load_failure/2` â†’ `abort_reason: "runtime_load_failed"` per r6). Spec line: `.spec/specs/cli.spec.md:292`.
Suggested fix: cast the ebin path before passing to OTP â€” change `:code.add_pathz(ebin)` at line 322 to `:code.add_pathz(to_charlist(ebin))` (one-line change; `Mix.Local.archive_ebin/1` returns a binary, `:code.add_pathz/1` requires a charlist).

Confirming: S2's deliverable (`test/integration/archive_install_test.exs`, the `mix.exs` `test.integration` alias edit, the `test_helper.exs` exclude addition, the `v11.execute: true` flip) is correct and merge-ready. Once the new S1b ticket lands the one-line fix in `lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:322`, S2's test will go green and this stage can merge.

SCOPE_EXPANSION: spawned mutagen-wrd.40.4 to fix the :code.add_pathz/1 charlist/binary mismatch at lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:322 that S2's end-to-end test exposed.

S2's test body (commit ae68d59c) is structurally correct and merge-ready. The failure surfaced by `mix test test/integration/archive_install_test.exs --include archive_integration` is in S1-owned code (lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex), which is in S2's Out-of-Scope (files) list. Per /orchestrate-epic skill's scope-expansion authority, the right routing is to carve out a new subticket rather than bounce S2 to fix code S2 is not allowed to touch.

mutagen-wrd.40.4 now blocks mutagen-wrd.40.2. S2 will pick up automatically once .40.4 closes.

This SCOPE_EXPANSION does NOT consume a bounce attempt â€” it is a re-plan, not a failure. S2 has used 1 of 2 bounces so far (the original mix-in-mix lock deadlock bounce, now resolved by the test redesign in ae68d59c).

MERGE_CONFLICT: rebase onto think-pvr/archive-install-fix (ebcd4e1) was clean (no file conflicts), but the integration test mix test test/integration/archive_install_test.exs --include archive_integration still fails. The original S2-exposed FunctionClauseError leak is GONE (S1b's charlist cast fixed it). New failure mode: runtime_load_failed because telemetry.app cannot be found from the installed archive. Output: {:error, {:telemetry, {~c"no such file or directory", ~c"telemetry.app"}}}. This is a real downstream code-path issue (archive install does not bring transitive deps' .app files into code path), not a rebase conflict. Stage branch left rebased at e8c04aa on top of ebcd4e1; no abort performed. make smoke NOT run because test failed first.

ORCHESTRATOR_ESCALATION â€” second product-level gap discovered, pausing for human direction.

After S1b (mutagen-wrd.40.4) landed and S2's branch rebased onto think-pvr/archive-install-fix at ebcd4e1:

1. The FunctionClauseError leak S2 originally exposed is GONE (S1b's `to_charlist(ebin)` cast worked).
2. `mix test test/integration/archive_install_test.exs --include archive_integration` still fails with a DIFFERENT, deeper error:
   ```
   {:error, {:telemetry, {~c"no such file or directory", ~c"telemetry.app"}}}
   ```
3. Root cause: `mix archive.build` from the mutagen_ex repo produces a .ez containing ONLY mutagen_ex's compiled beam files â€” NOT its transitive deps. mutagen_ex has exactly one runtime dep, `telemetry ~> 1.0` (mix.exs line ~50). When the archive-installed `mix mutagen` runs against a fresh `mix new` host that doesn't declare telemetry, telemetry.app is not on the code path, `Application.ensure_all_started(:mutagen_ex)` fails after S1's repair appends the archives directory, and S1's self-heal correctly falls through to the structured-error path (abort_reason: "runtime_load_failed"). The test's assertion `aborted == false` then trips.

This is a planning gap. The original /mini-plan and the three stage tickets (S1, S2, S3) all assumed `mix archive.install ./mutagen_ex-X.Y.Z.ez` followed by `mix mutagen` would work end-to-end. It currently cannot â€” not without one of:

**Option A â€” bundle deps into the archive**: Change how mutagen_ex is packaged so the .ez includes telemetry.beam/telemetry.app alongside mutagen_ex.beam. `mix archive.build` does not do this by default; would need a custom packaging step (or a different distribution mechanism, e.g. escript). Largest scope, but most user-friendly.

**Option B â€” extend the self-heal to load deps**: Have S1's repair code also scan for and add other ebin paths (e.g. mutagen_ex_deps/ alongside the main archive, or query Mix.path_for(:archives) for telemetry.ez). Requires the user to have separately installed `telemetry` as an archive too. Modest scope, but documentation burden.

**Option C â€” adjust the test scope**: The test's host project would `mix deps.get` telemetry into its own deps before invoking `mix mutagen`. This sidesteps the issue but breaks the s14c spec â€” "host that does NOT declare :mutagen_ex" was meant to imply "and does not declare its transitive deps either." Scope reduction.

**Option D â€” document the limitation**: Update README/CHANGELOG (S3 already merged â€” would need a follow-up) to state archive-install requires the host project to declare `telemetry ~> 1.0` as a dep, or to have telemetry installed as a separate archive. Smallest scope, but minimally repairs the original user-facing bug (since `mix mutagen` would still crash for a naive user).

**Option E â€” drop archive-install support**: Close mutagen-wrd.40 as wontfix; reopen S1/S2/S3 work as "not achievable without product change beyond planned scope." Largest scope reduction.

I will NOT carve another subticket autonomously. The first scope expansion (mutagen-wrd.40.4) was a clear one-line bug fix within S1's existing design; this is a design question. Human direction needed.

State at pause:
- mutagen-wrd.40.1 (S1): CLOSED at 5a958ce
- mutagen-wrd.40.3 (S3): CLOSED at 677a209
- mutagen-wrd.40.4 (S1b): CLOSED at ebcd4e1
- mutagen-wrd.40.2 (S2): OPEN, rebased to e8c04aa on think-pvr/archive-install-fix (epic HEAD ebcd4e1), test red as described above
- Epic branch think-pvr/archive-install-fix at ebcd4e1 â€” not yet pushed, no PR opened.
- Worktree /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-archive-install-test is intact for inspection.

APPROVED (user override). Merged into think-pvr/archive-install-fix at e8c04aa per direct user direction "merge and clean up" â€” overriding the prior ORCHESTRATOR_ESCALATION.

State of the deliverable at merge:
- Test body (Mix.Task.run in-process + scoped MIX_ARCHIVES + on_exit cleanup) is structurally correct and runs to a deterministic outcome.
- The test currently FAILS on the telemetry.app code-path gap documented in the prior ORCHESTRATOR_ESCALATION comment â€” that is a known limitation of the current archive-install design (mix archive.build doesn't bundle transitive deps), not a defect in this stage's test code.
- @moduletag :archive_integration excludes the test from default `mix test`, so `make smoke` remains green (640/0 confirmed post-merge).
- v11.execute is `true`. Any future `mix spec.check`-style runner that exercises the v11 stub will see it as red. Resolution path is a separate workstream (see the five options A-E in the ORCHESTRATOR_ESCALATION comment).

Closing per user sign-off.

title: S2: Archive-install integration test (mirrors downstream-adoption)
